title: Clean a Kitchen Counter
summary: Wipe down and sanitize a kitchen counter after food preparation.
learnerPromise: You will be able to clear, wipe, and sanitize a kitchen counter after cooking or preparing food.
whyItMatters: Kitchen counters can collect food residue and bacteria during meal prep. Cleaning them after each use helps keep your cooking area safe and prevents pests.
realLifeUses: Cleaning up after chopping vegetables for dinner
Wiping down the counter after making a sandwich
Sanitizing the surface after handling raw meat or eggs
Doing a quick clean after a baking session
materialsNeeded: Warm soapy water or kitchen cleaner
result: A clean, dry kitchen counter free of food residue and visible stains.
youWillLearn: Clear crumbs and debris from the counter
Wash the surface with soapy water
Rinse or wipe away soap residue
Dry the counter to prevent moisture buildup
miniChallenge: Clean your kitchen counter after your next meal prep. Remove all debris, wash with soapy water, and dry it completely.
steps: Remove all items from the counter. Put away ingredients, tools, and containers.
Scrape or wipe crumbs and debris into your hand or a trash bin.
Wash the counter with warm soapy water or a kitchen-safe cleaner. Wipe in one direction.
Rinse the cloth and wipe again to remove any soap or cleaner residue.
Dry the counter with a clean, dry cloth.
completionCriteria: The counter is clear of all food debris and items
The surface is clean with no visible residue or stickiness
The counter is dry after cleaning
commonProblems: Using the same cloth that was used for raw meat without washing it first
Leaving the counter wet, which can breed bacteria or damage surfaces
Pushing crumbs onto the floor instead of into the trash
tips: Clean as you go while cooking. It makes the final cleanup much faster.
Use a different cloth for raw meat areas than for general counters.
If you handled raw meat, eggs, or fish, sanitize the counter after washing it with soap.
